entries.title: Outrage over Israeli soldier's vandalism of Jesus statue in Lebanon
entries.snippet: The Israeli military says it views with "great severity" the actions of the soldier, as the US ambassador demands "swift" consequences.

entries.title: US singer D4vd pleads not guilty to murder in death of missing teen girl
entries.snippet: Prosecutors say the singer, real name David Anthony Burke, could face life in prison due to the nature of the case, if convicted.

entries.title: Japan on high alert for 'huge' second quake after issuing tsunami warning
entries.snippet: Japan's meteorological agency warns another, stronger earthquake may hit in the next week,

entries.title: What we know about the Iranian ship seized by the US
entries.snippet: The US has intercepted an Iranian ship entering the Gulf as part of its naval blockade, US President Donald Trump has said.

entries.title: Oil prices rise after Trump says Iranian ship seized
entries.snippet: Energy markets have seen wild swings since the US and Israel attacked Iran on 28 February.

entries.title: Man kills seven of his children, and an eighth child, in Louisiana mass shooting
entries.snippet: Three boys and five girls, aged between three to 11, were killed in the shooting on Sunday, officials say.

entries.title: More than 200 rescued from IS-linked group in DR Congo
entries.snippet: The youngest of those freed from an Allied Democratic Forces camp is a girl aged just 14, Uganda's army says.

entries.title: Police gunfight with favela gang traps 200 tourists on hilltop
entries.snippet: The access route to Morro Dois Irmãos was blocked during the operation in Vidigal, leaving scores of alarmed sightseers stuck on the hill.

entries.title: Trailblazing black beauty queen who defied apartheid South Africa dies aged 76
entries.snippet: At the 1972 Miss World contest, Cynthia Shange was one of two contestants from South Africa - one black and one white.

entries.title: New Zealand declares state of emergency in Wellington as floods hit
entries.snippet: Footage online shows vehicles submerged, trees uprooted and houses hit by landslides.

entries.title: Days of Our Lives and Starship Troopers actor Patrick Muldoon dies aged 57
entries.snippet: The actor and producer also appeared in Melrose Place and Saved By the Bell.

entries.title: Elon Musk snubs interview summons by French prosecutors amid X probe
entries.snippet: The Paris prosecutor's office told the BBC it had "taken note of the absence of the people summoned".

entries.title: Iranian woman arrested in US for allegedly trafficking arms to Sudan
entries.snippet: Shamim Mafi is accused of brokering the sales of arms to Sudan's defence ministry on behalf of Iran.

entries.title: Rumen Radev's party wins Bulgarian election
entries.snippet: With 87% of the vote now counted, Progressive Bulgaria has secured a majority in parliament.

entries.title: Rebel Wilson's claims against actress are 'malicious concoctions', Australian court hears
entries.snippet: Wilson has been accused of defaming the star of her film The Deb over an incident involving a bath in Bondi.

entries.title: Ukraine police chief resigns after officers allegedly fled deadly shooting
entries.snippet: Authorities say the officers have been suspended and an investigation into their actions is under way.

entries.title: At least 25 killed in firecracker factory blast in India
entries.snippet: Mostly female workers have been killed in the blast, which occurred in Tamil Nadu state.

entries.title: Thousands of Parisians evacuated as WW2 bomb detonated
entries.snippet: Residents within a 450m radius of the device were told to leave their homes while it was neutralised.

entries.title: Rat poison found in HiPP baby food jar in Austria, police say
entries.snippet: The brand warned on Saturday that consuming the purées may be potentially "life-threatening".

entries.title: Ontario Premier Doug Ford agrees to sell $21m private jet following outcry
entries.snippet: After confirming the purchase on Friday, Ford drew critcism from political opponents who called it a "gravy plane" and "out of touch".

entries.title: The Onion has agreed to a new deal to take over Infowars
entries.snippet: The Onion says it has a new deal to take over conspiracy theorist Alex Jones's Infowars media company. If approved, the satirical news website could turn Infowars into a parody of itself.

entries.title: Tim Cook to step down as Apple CEO. In letter, describes 15 years of emails
entries.snippet: The 65-year-old Cook will turn over CEO duties to Apple's head of hardware products, John Ternus, in September. Cook will remain with the company as executive chairman.

entries.title: Trump's labor secretary resigns amid investigation into misconduct
entries.snippet: Labor Secretary Lori Chavez-DeRemer, who was under internal investigation, is leaving her position. She becomes the third cabinet departure of President Trump's second term.

entries.title: The 11 most challenged books of 2025, according to the American Library Association
entries.snippet: The ALA says 4,235 titles were challenged at U.S. libraries — the second-highest year on record. Forty percent of the challenged works involved LGBTQ+ subjects or the experiences of people of color.

entries.title: Peace talks are in doubt as the U.S. seizes an Iranian ship
entries.snippet: President Trump said a U.S. delegation will head to Pakistan to resume talks to end the war with Iran, but Tehran expressed reluctance after the U.S. seized one of its cargo ships in the Strait of Hormuz.

entries.title: A mine despoiled the beauty of the rainforest. This Goldman Prize winner took action
entries.snippet: "We women are the land guardians and keepers," says Theonila Roka Matbob of Papua New Guinea, recognized for her efforts to repair the environmental and social harms caused by a copper and gold mine.

entries.title: U.S. seizes Iranian cargo ship. And, tariff refund portal launches
entries.snippet: U.S. forces seized an Iranian-flagged cargo vessel in the Strait of Hormuz. And, an online government portal for processing tariff refunds launches today.

entries.title: The Sonoran Desert teems with wildlife. These 3D scans could help protect its future
entries.snippet: A new art exhibit in Phoenix features some of the world's prickliest plants. It could also help save them.

entries.title: Spanish speakers learn strategies to pass English-only driving test in Florida
entries.snippet: A new English-only driving test rule in Florida is fueling a surge in strategy lessons for Spanish speakers where they learn to figure out the questions without having to take English language courses.

entries.title: Who owns presidential records? Trump's Justice Department says it's him
entries.snippet: The Trump administration asserts that a nearly 50-year-old law requiring the preservation of presidential records is unconstitutional. Historians warn important papers could be destroyed.

entries.title: Next round of U.S.-Iran talks in doubt as tensions rise ahead of ceasefire deadline
entries.snippet: An American delegation, led by Vice President Vance, will soon leave for Pakistan to resume talks with Iran over the nearly two-month-long war. Iranian officials have been noncommittal about attending the round of talks. President Trump also sent mixed signals, predicting success

entries.title: Retired admiral breaks down U.S. strategy behind naval blockade
entries.snippet: To discuss the U.S. shooting and disabling of an Iranian cargo ship to enforce its naval blockade, Geoff Bennett spoke with retired Adm. Gary Roughead. He was Chief of Naval Operations from 2007 to 2011 and is now a Distinguished Military Fellow at the Hoover Institution at Stanf

entries.title: Middle East experts analyze U.S. and Iran priorities in potential negotiations
entries.snippet: For additional perspective on the state of diplomacy with Iran, Amna Nawaz spoke with Alan Eyre and Miad Maleki. Eyre was part of the Obama administration's negotiating team for the Iran nuclear deal and is now at the Middle East Institute. Maleki was born and raised in Iran and

entries.title: News Wrap: Patel sues The Atlantic over story alleging excessive drinking and absences
entries.snippet: In our news wrap Monday, FBI Director Kash Patel filed a lawsuit against The Atlantic magazine over a story alleging "excessive drinking and unexplained absences," Monday marked the first day businesses could submit refund requests for tariffs paid before they were ruled unconsti

entries.title: Killing of 8 children in Louisiana renews focus on domestic violence involving guns
entries.snippet: Another deadly shooting has torn apart another American community. In Shreveport, Louisiana, a man shot and killed eight children and left two other adults gravely wounded. The killer was reportedly the father of seven of the eight children he shot. William Brangham discussed the

entries.title: Redistricting battle reaches Virginia as voters weigh new congressional map
entries.snippet: In Virginia, voters are heading to the polls on Tuesday for a high-stakes ballot measure that could reshape the state's congressional map and potentially shift the balance of power in Washington. Supporters say it's a necessary response to aggressive Republican-led redistricting

entries.title: Tamara Keith and Amy Walter on partisan redistricting and the midterms
entries.snippet: NPR's Tamara Keith and Amy Walter of the Cook Political Report with Amy Walter join Geoff Bennett to discuss the latest political news, including Labor Secretary Lori Chavez-DeRemer leaving her post, partisan redistricting and the midterms and Democratic fundraising advantages.

entries.title: Cities turn streetlights red to protect nocturnal ecosystems
entries.snippet: International Dark Sky Week is hailed as a global celebration of the night and a call for less light pollution. In some cities internationally, local governments are trying to find a solution to lighting the night can better serve critical wildlife ecosystems and humans alike. Ma

entries.title: Lori Chavez-DeRemer is out as labor secretary, White House says
entries.snippet: Chavez-DeRemer is the third Trump Cabinet member to leave her post in recent months.

entries.title: Apple CEO Tim Cook to step down, ending nearly 15-year reign leading the iPhone maker
entries.snippet: Cook will turn the CEO duties to Apple's head of hardware engineering, John Tenus, on Sept. 1 while remaining involved with the company as executive chairman.

entries.title: Four U.N. secretary-general candidates audition this week. That's far fewer than in 2016
entries.snippet: Chile's former President Michelle Bachelet — one of two women and one of three from Latin America — will be the first to face ambassadors from the U.N.'s 193 member nations during a three-hour question-and-answer session Tuesday.

entries.title: Mexico's Sheinbaum demands explanation after U.S. officials die assisting in operation in Chihuahua
entries.snippet: Mexican President Claudia Sheinbaum says she was unaware of any collaboration between the U.S. and local authorities in Chihuahua after four officials died in an accident over the weekend.

entries.title: Gunman at Mexico's Teotihuacán pyramids kills 1 Canadian tourist, injures 6
entries.snippet: A man with a gun opened fire Monday at the historic Teotihuacán pyramids, killing one Canadian tourist and injuring six other people at the tourist site an hour north of Mexico's capital, the Mexican government said.

entries.title: A long-shuttered Iraq-Syria border crossing reopens for the first time in more than a decade
entries.snippet: A key border crossing between Iraq and Syria reopened on Monday for the first time in more than a decade, with officials highlighting its potential for trade and oil exports.

entries.title: AP sources: Witnesses subpoenaed to testify before D.C. grand jury in John Brennan investigation
entries.snippet: The Justice Department has subpoenaed several witnesses to testify before a federal grand jury in Washington as part of its investigation into former CIA Director John Brennan.

entries.title: Actors reenact Paul Revere's historic midnight ride with some modern-day tweaks
entries.snippet: Revere's ride took place on April 18, 1775, when the silversmith and express rider was dispatched to Lexington to warn Revolutionary leaders Samuel Adams and John Hancock that the British were coming to arrest them.

entries.title: FBI Director Kash Patel sues The Atlantic for article that alleged excessive drinking
entries.snippet: FBI Director Kash Patel has filed a $250 million lawsuit against The Atlantic magazine and its reporter Sarah Fitzpatrick, contending its article posted last week that talked about his alleged excessive drinking was a "malicious hit piece."

entries.title: Migrants rush to apply under Spain's new mass legalization program
entries.snippet: Spain has launched a mass legalization measure for migrants, allowing them to apply in-person for legal status. It contrasts with other European countries that are tightening immigration controls.

entries.title: Oil prices rise and stocks give back part of record-breaking rally following latest Iran tensions
entries.snippet: Oil prices are climbing following the latest rise of U.S.-Iran tensions, but the moves are more modest than they were earlier in the war.

entries.title: Singer D4vd charged with murder of 14-year-old found decomposed in his car
entries.snippet: Los Angeles prosecutors said Monday that the 21-year-old singer whose legal name is David Burke was charged with first-degree murder in the killing of Celeste Rivas Hernandez.

entries.title: DW
entries.snippet: News, Analysis and Service from Germany and Europe - in 30 Languages

entries.title: Tim Cook to step down as Apple CEO, John Ternus to take over
entries.snippet: John Ternus, who has long focused ​on design and ‌products at Apple, will lead the iPhone maker as it gears up for an industry change spurred by artificial intelligence.

entries.title: Mexico: Canadian killed in shooting at Teotihuacan pyramid
entries.snippet: A gunman opened fire at the popular tourist and archeological site outside Mexico city.

entries.title: UK: Starmer says he would't have appointed Mandelson as US envoy if he'd known he failed vetting process
entries.snippet: Under-fire Prime Minister Keir Starmer told the House of Commons that neither he nor his ministers were told that the former ambassador to the US and Epstein associate Peter Mandelson had failed his security vetting.

entries.title: Elon Musk skips probe as France investigates X
entries.snippet: French prosecutors are investigating whether content generated by X's AI chatbot Grok contributed to the spread of child sexual abuse material, explicit deepfakes and Holocaust denial.

entries.title: Ugandan military reports rescue of some 200 hostages from ADF militants in eastern DRC
entries.snippet: Uganda's military has said that it and forces from the neighboring Democratic Republic of Congo freed some 200 hostages in eastern DRC in a joint operation against Islamist militants known as the ADF.

entries.title: Unpacking the 2026 Venice Biennale controversies and highlights
entries.snippet: Russia's return to the international contemporary art exhibition, as well as the participation of Israel and the US, have sparked politicized backlash.

entries.title: Sudan war enters fourth year as child hunger crisis deepens
entries.snippet: Sudan’s war has displaced millions, leaving parts of the country facing famine. Aid agencies warn children are bearing the brunt as food shortages worsen and humanitarian funding declines.

entries.title: Is To Lam's Vietnam turning into Xi Jinping's China?
entries.snippet: After Vietnam's lawmakers unanimously elected Communist Party chief To Lam as the country's president, analysts are asking whether Vietnam aims to copy China's political model.

entries.title: Bundesliga debut by female coach marred by misogyny
entries.snippet: Marie-Louise Eta's historic debut as a head coach in the men's Bundesliga ended in defeat. While her appointment is a sign of progress, the reactions to it leave no doubt about how much is still to be done.

entries.title: South Korea, India agree to boost trade ties amid global tensions
entries.snippet: Prime Minister Narendra Modi hosts South Korean President Lee Jae Myung this week as both countries target $50 billion in trade by 2030.

entries.title: Bulgaria enters uncharted territory as Radev wins big
entries.snippet: Preliminary results show that former President Rumen Radev has won Bulgaria's general election with a clear majority and the biggest result for a single party since 1997. Will Bulgaria finally get a stable government?

entries.title: How China's chip expansion puts pressure on global rivals
entries.snippet: US curbs on advanced chips pushed China to focus on its own semiconductor ecosystem. And while it trails at the very cutting edge, China's "good‑enough" technology is fast powering much of the global economy.

entries.title: Japan issues tsunami alert after strong earthquake
entries.snippet: The 7.4-magnitude quake off northern Iwate prefecture could be felt as far as Tokyo. Residents have been urged to evacuate northern coastal regions ahead of a tsunami.

entries.title: Bulgaria: Pro-Russia Rumen Radev wins ‌election
entries.snippet: Rumen Radev's new center-left party, Progressive Bulgaria has won an absolute majority in the country's eighth general election in five years. Both Russia and the EU have already sent their congratulations.

entries.title: Ukraine hits Russian Black Sea port city, 1 dead
entries.snippet: Russian authorities said one man was killed in a Ukrainian drone raid on city of Tuapse. Meanwhile, Russia arrested a German woman in an alleged bomb plot.

entries.title: Iran war: 'No plans' for fresh peace talks, Tehran says
entries.snippet: Pakistan is preparing to host a second round of negotiations, but doubts remain as to whether they will take place, after the US seizure of an Iranian cargo ship. DW has the latest.

entries.title: US strike on alleged drug boat in Caribbean kills 3
entries.snippet: Trump has said that the US is in "armed conflict" with cartels, as its military campaign has killed at least 180 people since last year amid criticism over its legality.

entries.title: Germany news: Timmy the stranded whale back on the move
entries.snippet: The struggling humpback whale is due to be fitted with a radio transmitter to help follow his recovery. Meanwhile, Chancellor Merz has promised that Germany is prepared for any crisis.

entries.title: Will Venezuela's oil sector reform attract investors?
entries.snippet: After the US ousted Maduro and effectively seized control of Venezuela's oil industry, the country's economy has been transitioning rapidly. The interim government is wooing investors but lacks democratic backing.
